name: CALERIE caloric restriction slowed DunedinPACE but not clock age estimates
summary: The strongest controlled caloric-restriction methylation evidence is a small DunedinPACE slowing signal with null findings on several biological-age clocks.
statement: In the randomized CALERIE trial, caloric restriction slowed DunedinPACE over two years but did not significantly change DNA-methylation biological-age estimates including PhenoAge and GrimAge.
quantitative_note: The abstract characterizes the DunedinPACE treatment effect as small and notes non-significant changes in PhenoAge and GrimAge.
caveats: This was a post hoc DNA-methylation analysis rather than the original primary clinical endpoint.
The signal was specific to pace-of-aging rather than reversal in biological-age clocks.
Effects were small and overlapping between intervention and control participants.
CALERIE does not establish that methylation changes mediate healthspan or lifespan benefit.
